sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: Born in 1953, Canadian scientist Dr Laura Prentiss has an (1) ...... (ENDURE) legacy in the environmental science community for her groundbreaking work on clean energy.
She was the researcher who (2) ...... (REVOLT) our understanding of solar panel efficiency,
and she also proposed a radical (3) ...... (ALTER) to fossil fuels that sparked considerable debate across the scientific world.
Her original battery design, and its greatly improved (4) ...... (PLACE), the GridCell Pro, became essential components in renewable energy grids around the globe.
Despite the limited (5) ...... (CAPABLE) of early prototypes, they gave remote communities reliable access to power and even enabled local (6) ...... (PROCESS) of sustainable resources.
The GridCell Pro, a compact unit powered entirely by (7) ...... (ELECTRIC), was launched in 1996 and represented Prentiss's bold effort to (8) ...... (MODERN) the way communities stored energy.

However, the first commercial model was (9) ...... (PERSIST) criticised by investors for being too costly to manufacture on a large scale, and full production was (10) ...... (CONTINUE) in the spring of 2001.

sections.title: B: Multiple Choice (Grammar)
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: When I **spotted / was spotting** Laura, she was jogging along the riverside path.
Emma wasn't surprised by the news because she **already read / had already read** the report.
He was embarrassed because it was the first time he **tasted / had tasted** such spicy food.
As a teenager, my neighbour **would / used to** collect rare stamps from all over the world.
I **was / have been** so relieved when the exam results finally came out.
She **was always borrowing / had always borrowed** our tools and then forgetting to return them.
The puppy **chased / had been chasing** its tail all morning, so it was completely exhausted by lunchtime.
After three months at sea, the sailors **were used to sleeping / used to sleep** in narrow bunks.

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
Don't you think we should tidy the office before the inspection? **up**
Isn't it about time ...... the office before the inspection?

Why should I give you suggestions if you are just going to ignore them? **use**
What's ...... you suggestions if you are just going to ignore them?

The rehabilitation programme appears to have completely transformed Danny's attitude. **leaf**
Danny has ...... he completed the rehabilitation programme.
items.answer: turned over a new leaf since

items.sentence:
There's no point moaning to me about it since nothing is going to change. **good**
It's ...... it since nothing is going to change.
items.answer: no good complaining to me about

items.sentence:
You have very little time left to submit this report, so you need to focus. **clock**
You are ...... , so you need to focus.

When Marco's dream of climbing Everest finally came true, he was completely overwhelmed. **reality**
Marco was completely overwhelmed when his lifelong dream of climbing Everest ...... .

I often think that Rachel is actually her identical twin sister, Paula. **mix**
I often ...... her identical twin sister, Paula.

Would you ever want to live the life of a professional athlete? **places**
Would you ever want to ...... a professional athlete?

sections.title: D: Gap Fill (Prepositions / Collocations)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Rodriguez was ………………… the lead for the entire race until she stumbled near the finish line.
I'm afraid the laboratory is ………………… use at the moment, but you are welcome to wait outside.
The new library card gives all students free access ………………… thousands of digital publications.
The physiotherapist confirmed that the athlete's condition had changed ………………… the better after two weeks of treatment.
Due to unforeseen circumstances, the ceremony will be rescheduled and held ………………… a later date.
Not all the sources you come across ………………… the Web are reliable or accurate.
I'm sure Lucas didn't mean to upset you; he couldn't have done it ………………… purpose.
The young swimmer has been ………………… excellent form all season and is tipped to break the record.
When Sophie rang, she sounded as though she ………………… some very distressing news.
At the very moment you knocked on the door, I ………………… ready to leave the house.
Social media has had a massive ………………… on the way teenagers communicate with one another.
Strange as it may sound, last night was actually the first time I ………………… a home-cooked Moroccan meal.
Make sure you ………………… up your files regularly, as your hard drive could fail at any time.
The journalist was accused of ………………… the facts to make the story appear more dramatic.
That modern sculpture looks a bit out of ………………… in the old town square, don't you think?
She missed her early flight because she ………………… about packing all night and ended up oversleeping.
